title: Perunthenaruvi Falls
tagline: Perunthenaruvi Falls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Perunthenaruvi Waterfalls are waterfalls 36 km (22 mi) from Pathanamthitta in Pathanamthitta District, Central Travancore region, Kerala State, India.
overview.description: Perunthenaruvi Waterfalls are waterfalls 36 km (22 mi) from Pathanamthitta in Pathanamthitta District, Central Travancore region, Kerala State, India. It is a popular tourist destination situated in Vechoochira Panchayat of Ranni taluk. The one shore of this waterfall is Kudamurutty and Vechoochira is the other. The main route to this waterfall starts from Ranni - Athikkayam - Kudamurutty - Perunthenaruvi.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Perunthenaruvi Weir
topPlaces.description: Perunthenaruvi Weir is a diversion dam built across Pamba river at villages of Naranammoozhy and Vechoochira in Pathanamthitta District of Kerala, India. Perumthenaruvi Small Hydroelectric Project 6 MW (2×3) is a run-of-the-river scheme in river Pamba, and the weir is a part of this scheme.

topPlaces.name: Chathanthara
topPlaces.description: Chathanthara is a small village in Vechoochira panchayath of Ranni taluk, Kerala state, India. It is about 10 km (6.2 mi) from Erumely, 18 km (11 mi) from Ranni and 7 km (4.3 mi) from Vechoochira.

topPlaces.name: Kollamula
topPlaces.description: Kollamula is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. It is one of the 11 villages in Ranni taluk.

topPlaces.name: Athikkayam
topPlaces.description: Athikkayam is a village located in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala State, India. It is a small village that is situated on the bank of the Pamba River It's one of the 11 villages in Ranni taluk.

topPlaces.name: Vechoochira
topPlaces.description: Vechoochira is a village located in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India. The nearest Railway Station is Thiruvalla, located 45km away from the village.

topPlaces.name: Mukkoottuthara
topPlaces.description: Mukkoottuthara is a developing town located in the south eastern part of Kottayam district of Kerala State, India. Sabarimala is only 48 km (30 mi) from Erumely while travelling through Mukkoottuthara.

topPlaces.name: Naranammoozhy
topPlaces.description: Naranammoozhy is a village and panchayat within Ranni taluk of Pathanamthitta district in Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalaketty
topPlaces.description: Kalaketty is a village in the Erumely panchayath of Kanjirappally tehsil in the Kottayam district of Kerala state, India. Many Shiva devotees pass through Kalaketty during the Makaravilakku festival period, as the local temple is an important landmark as well as an idathavalam for them.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Perunthenaruvi Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Perunthenaruvi Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Perunthenaruvi Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (97 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Perunthenaruvi Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Perunthenaruvi Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Perunthenaruvi Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Perunthenaruvi Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Perunthenaruvi Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Perunthenaruvi Weir, Chathanthara, Kollamula, Athikkayam, Vechoochira.
